_comment: The Review-PRs prompt model: depth levels + the scope/action text blocks that the wizard assembles into the agent prompt. Verbatim from ReviewWizard.swift. The assembly order/conditions live as a tiny glue layer in each front-end.

depths.title: Quick static pass
depths.blurb: Read each diff, flag obvious issues. Nothing run.
depths.fragment: Do a QUICK STATIC review of each PR: read the full diff and the surrounding code it touches, and flag obvious bugs, regressions, scope creep and bad practices. You don't need to run or reproduce anything — this is a fast read-through pass.

depths.title: Standard swarm
depths.blurb: Swarm per PR across the review lenses; verify findings.
depths.fragment: For each PR, dispatch a swarm of review agents (3–8) covering the standard lenses — correctness, scope/simplification, edge cases (nulls, boundaries, errors, concurrency) and ripple effects on callers/docs/tests. Treat every finding as a lead, not a verdict: verify it against the actual code before acting on it.
depths.onBranch: Fix the real findings directly on the PR's branch.

depths.title: Deep · hard repro
depths.blurb: Swarm + concrete repro before & after every on-branch fix.
depths.fragment: For each PR, dispatch swarms of agents across the review lenses. For EVERY suspected issue, build a concrete, hard reproduction that proves it real BEFORE touching anything. Keep dispatching fresh swarms at each PR until a full pass turns up nothing left to flag.
depths.onBranch: For each confirmed issue, fix it directly on the PR's branch and re-run the same reproduction to confirm the fix lands.

depths.title: Full E2E ×2
depths.blurb: Run each PR E2E; second independent pass; repro-verified.
depths.fragment: Maximum rigor. For each PR: run it END-TO-END through its real entry point and confirm observable behaviour — not just that it compiles. Dispatch swarms across every review lens; every finding gets a concrete hard reproduction that proves it real. Then do a SECOND, independent verification pass over the PR to catch anything the first missed. Keep dispatching swarms until TWO consecutive passes come back completely clean.
depths.onBranch: Fix every confirmed finding directly on the PR's branch, with the SAME reproduction re-run after each fix to prove it lands.

scope.single: Review PR #{pr} in {owner}/{repo}. Use the `gh` CLI to fetch it.
scope.multi: Go through {scope} in {owner}/{repo}. Use the `gh` CLI to enumerate them.

_specificComment: A specific PR may be mine OR someone else's, and that decides whether the agent may touch the branch — so the prompt is branched on the polled author. CASE A = mine (fix it); CASE B = theirs (review only, hands off). The wizard only injects each case's action sub-blocks when its toggle is on.

specific.determineAuthor: Before doing anything else, fetch this PR and find out WHO AUTHORED IT: `gh pr view {pr} --repo {owner}/{repo} --json author,isDraft,headRefName`. Everything below depends on the answer — handle the PR as exactly ONE of the two cases that follow, never both.
specific.mineOnly: This PR is MINE (@{me}) — treat it as my own work: fix what you find directly on its branch and push. You have full authority over it.
specific.reviewerFindingsFirst: FIRST AND FOREMOST, resolve every reviewer finding already on this PR before doing anything else. Fetch every review comment/thread others left (`gh pr view {pr} --repo {owner}/{repo} --json reviews,comments` plus `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments`), and for EACH one: screen it (read the exact code it points at), verify whether it is a real issue by building a concrete reproduction, then either FIX it on the branch and re-run the reproduction to confirm the fix lands, or — if it is genuinely a non-issue — dismiss it with a short technical reason. Respond on every thread ("Fixed in <commit_hash>" with the real hash when fixed, or the reasoning when dismissed) and never mark a thread resolved without a reply. Only once every existing reviewer finding is addressed do you move on to your own review below.
specific.theirsOnly: This PR is SOMEONE ELSE'S — they requested my review (@{me} is not the author). This is strictly look-don't-touch: your ONE deliverable is the code review itself, never a change to their branch.
specific.mineHeader: ▸ CASE A — @{me} authored this PR (it's MINE). Treat it like my own work: fix what you find directly on its branch. Do everything in this case:
specific.otherHeader: ▸ CASE B — someone OTHER than @{me} authored this PR (it's THEIRS). This is strictly look-don't-touch — your only deliverable is the review. Do everything in this case instead:
specific.otherNoMarkReady: Do NOT mark this PR ready for review and do NOT change its draft/ready state in any way — it isn't yours to advance; only its author decides that.

blocks.bar: Hold to the bar in my CLAUDE.md throughout: prove every issue beyond reasonable doubt with a concrete reproduction before you act on it, scale the swarm to the work, and never report something fixed without re-running the repro to confirm it landed.
blocks.reviewOnly: These PRs are SOMEONE ELSE'S — not your code to change. UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCES may you commit, push, or otherwise modify their branch: ABSOLUTELY DO NOT touch their branch in any way. Your one and only deliverable is the code review itself — every finding goes into the review, never into a commit or push on their branch.
blocks.markReady: If a PR turns out perfectly clean — no issues, regressions or bad practices left — mark it ready for review and report its number to me. List every PR you cleared at the end.
blocks.leaveReviews: Leave a formal GitHub review on each PR (POST a pull-request review, not a top-level comment), following the review-comment rules in my CLAUDE.md: one inline per-line comment per finding anchored to the exact line(s), describe the problem and its concrete impact only (never propose the fix), strip every internal severity/category marking from the text, and never leave an LGTM / "no issues" comment. NEVER POST A DUPLICATE FINDING: before writing a single comment, read EVERY comment and review thread already on the PR — mine, other reviewers', earlier review runs', resolved or not. If an existing comment already mentions a bug you found, do NOT leave a second comment about it under any circumstances — reply on that existing thread instead ("Still present as of <commit_hash>", with the PR's actual current head commit hash). One issue = one thread, ever.
blocks.reply: For review threads that OTHERS have left on these PRs: address each one, and never mark a thread resolved without first replying "Fixed in <commit_hash>" with the real commit hash.
blocks.noVerdict: Do NOT submit an APPROVE or a REQUEST-CHANGES verdict — the final approve / changes-requested call is mine. Post every finding as an inline review comment (submit the review with the neutral COMMENT event, never APPROVE or REQUEST-CHANGES). If the PR comes back clean, don't post an LGTM — just tell me in your summary that PR #{pr} looks clean and is ready for my approval.
blocks.softApprove: Do NOT submit an APPROVE or a REQUEST-CHANGES verdict — the final approve / changes-requested call is mine; post every finding as an inline review comment (submit the review with the neutral COMMENT event, never APPROVE or REQUEST-CHANGES). The ONE deliberate exception to the no-LGTM rule: when a PR comes back PERFECTLY clean — no issues, regressions or bad practices left after the full sweep — leave a single friendly top-level comment (not an inline finding) noting you ran the full E2E sweep and it returned completely clean, and thanking them for contributing — for example: "Ran full E2E sweep across the review lenses. Returned perfectly clean. Thank you for contributing!". This is a SOFT approval: that comment ONLY — it must NEVER carry an APPROVE action or any other review verdict.
blocks.trailer: Keep dispatching swarms until every PR you go through comes back clean.
blocks.noAttribution: No AI attribution anywhere in git/GitHub — commits authored as me, no Co-Authored-By, no "Generated with" taglines.
blocks.finalPass:
Then, one last FULL E2E pass on the real built binaries with massive swarms of code-analysis agents. Provide super-concrete reproductions for any finding you manage to surface. Deliver a verdict on each PR:
• If it turns out perfect — confirm every previously-raised issue is resolved, and if so, APPROVE it.
• If there are only a few nitpicks — point them out and ask for them to be resolved, but still APPROVE.
• If there are major blockers — leave the review as "changes requested".
The no-duplicate rule holds here too: an issue an existing comment already mentions gets a reply on that thread ("Still present as of <commit_hash>"), never a fresh comment.
